name: Bibliomancy ob3ect: divination by random opening of a sacred or significant book, letting the finger fall on a passage. FSPLIT: the opening of the book splits the unified text into the single passage revealed — the universe selects one fragment from the whole. FFUSE: the querent interprets the passage in the context of their question, reconstituting the random fragment as meaningful answer. VINIT: the closed book. TANCH: the book's covers — the boundary of all possible answers. AFWD: opening the book (forward in the text). AREV: flipping pages backward. ENGAGR: the passage is simultaneously random (stochastic selection) and destined (the universe chose it).

phase_0.domain_name: Bibliomancy ob3ect: divination by random opening of a sacred or significant book, letting the finger fall on a passage. FSPLIT: the opening of the book splits the unified text into the single passage revealed — the universe selects one fragment from the whole. FFUSE: the querent interprets the passage in the context of their question, reconstituting the random fragment as meaningful answer. VINIT: the closed book. TANCH: the book's covers — the boundary of all possible answers. AFWD: opening the book (forward in the text). AREV: flipping pages backward. ENGAGR: the passage is simultaneously random (stochastic selection) and destined (the universe chose it).

VINIT.justification: the unopened book is void of revealed meaning

TANCH.justification: they bound the set of all possible passages

AFWD.justification: forward motion through the text toward a passage

AREV.justification: reverse motion to a previous section

CLINK.justification: the act of composing the passage from words read in order

IMSCRIB.justification: the question is the self-referential identity that frames the reading

FSPLIT.justification: splits the unified text into the single revealed passage versus the rest of the book

FFUSE.justification: reconstitutes the random fragment as a meaningful answer in context of the question

EVALT.chosen_element: the passage clearly answers the question
EVALT.justification: affirmative state where meaning is evident

EVALF.justification: negative state where no connection is found

ENGAGR.chosen_element: the passage is both random and destined
ENGAGR.justification: paradox of stochastic selection and divine choice held simultaneously

IFIX.chosen_element: the written record of the reading
IFIX.justification: the passage and interpretation are permanently recorded

phase_2.split_input: the unified text of the book
phase_2.fuse_result: meaningful answer in context of the question

phase_3.both_description: passage is both random and destined

steps.domain_action: Step 1 IMSCRIB: the querent holds their question in mind
Step 2 AREV:   the querent reflects on the question's background
Step 3 FSPLIT: the book is opened, splitting text into passage and remainder
Step 4 AFWD:   the finger moves forward to land on a passage
Step 5 FFUSE:  the querent interprets the passage in context of the question
Step 6 CLINK:  the passage is read sequentially word by word
Step 7 IFIX:   the reading is recorded in a journal
Step 8 IMSCRIB: the querent recognizes the answer as their own

phase_5.compiler_frontend: the querent's intention to seek guidance
phase_5.ipc_mechanism: the physical turning of pages and finger placement
phase_5.memory_mechanism: the book's text and the querent's memory of the question
phase_5.scheduler_mechanism: the moment of opening determined by impulse or ritual
phase_5.alfs_store: the sacred book itself as canonical reference

phase_6.cycle_cost: the energy of opening the book and reading
phase_6.post_cycle_state: open book, passage selected, interpretation made
phase_6.delta_s_verdict: ΔS ≈ 0 — the system cycles between closed and open states without net disorder
